title: Fix buildah commit compress by default
body:
The change to switch over to cobra had the effect of always setting
compress to imagebuildah.Uncompressed. This is because the conditional
to set it to Gzip always returns false regardless of whether
--disable-compression is set or not.

This change fixes that, and make the logic match the intent of Gzip
being the default option.

comments.body: bud.go has the exact same issue, let me see if there are any other obvious cases of this
push.go does the right thing, I'll make bud.go and commit.go consistent with that.

@steveb I've enough tea in me today, and the change looks good.  However I think we've an issue where disable-compression is being set to false by default.  I'm going to spin another PR up for that.
LGTM

:pushpin: Commit 3a1034b has been approved by `TomSweeneyRedHat`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=TomSweeneyRedHat 3a1034b3eba4297a922137cf740fb1c06d136e5f -->

Approved by: TomSweeneyRedHat
Pushing e4c4d465bff217c1101b672d9eae0bc507667a35 to master...

I believe this is wrong, after talking to @nalind We should default all "commits" to uncompressed and only compress on buildah push.

Whether the user is calling buildah bud or buildah commit directly we should not be compressing.  Only compress when sending image over the internet.
